one_liner: NVDA pulling back to RSI 34 support zone with bearish MACD — sitting at mid-range between $175 support and $230 resistance; DCF base case of $334 offers massive upside but near-term momentum has stalled.

news_catalyst.headline: NVIDIA consolidating after AI infrastructure boom; no major new catalysts today
news_catalyst.summary: NVDA is down 1.6% in quiet trading with RSI at 34 approaching oversold territory. No major company-specific news today — the stock is digesting gains from recent AI infrastructure enthusiasm. The stock is in neutral Wyckoff consolidation between $175 support and $230 resistance. A hold signal is active with entry range $189-$195 and target $213.

technical.summary: NVDA is in neutral Wyckoff consolidation with no accumulation or distribution signals detected. RSI at 34.5 is approaching oversold territory which could provide a bounce level. MACD is in a bearish crossover on below-average volume. Watch for a hold or reversal at the $175-180 support zone — a break below would be bearish; a reclaim of $200 would re-open the path to resistance at $230.

valuation.summary: DCF base case of $334 implies 73% upside from current price at the maximum 15% discount rate with 50% growth assumption — compelling long-term value. Bear case at $192 is essentially the current price, limiting downside.

key_concerns: Valuation depends on sustained AI capex cycle
fundamentals.summary: Grade A fundamentals with dominant market position in AI accelerators. High leverage is the only flag — offset by exceptional cash generation. Long-term thesis intact; current pullback is a cooling period, not a breakdown.

risks: Break below $175 support would signal a deeper correction toward $150
Bearish MACD crossover with no accumulation Wyckoff signals — no structural floor confirmed
AI capex cycle slowdown or export restriction escalation could compress multiples
